mentions.note: Apple's AI deal with Alphabet makes Google the default search engine and extends Gemini integration. Neutral to positive for Alphabet as a beneficiary of AI deals.

Equity issuance to fund unprofitable data-center buildout raises dilution fears. Though $920M/month SpaceX deal shows Google Cloud customers prefer Nvidia GPUs over internal TPUs—a bullish signal for Nvidia offset by equity-supply concerns.
Just completed multi-billion fund raise for AI. Supply wave will impact stock in near term as investors raise cash.
Successfully raised $45B secondary offering with Goldman Sachs underwriting; huge demand, few flippers; stock bought back $45B last year, now selling $15B—capital deployment shift.

Antitrust fears overblown; appeal reversed monopoly ruling, retained $20B Apple search deal and Chrome. Gemini 3 best-in-class AI; YouTube leads media; Waymo robo-taxi leader. 28x forward earnings reasonable. Cramer bought back for Charitable Trust near end of year. Closed down $5 on day, finishing at $222.
Up 65% in 2025; antitrust trial amounted to slap on wrist. Gemini 3 AI platform threatening ChatGPT for mind share.
Up 3% YTD, continuing magical run from Q4 2025; owned for Investing Club trust; breaking free from MAG7 weakness.
Gemini 3 regarded as best-in-class generative AI; solved search-cannibalization concern; gaining enterprise and consumer traction vs. OpenAI.
Only Magnificent 7 stock currently strong; Cramer expects money to flow back to most of the seven eventually because they have too many levers and too much capital to bet against.

Best stock in the business by Cramer's count; many wrote off as worst Magnificent Seven but Gemini proved superior; owns YouTube, Waymo self-driving, Google Search—multiple competitive moats; recent buyback into Charitable Trust.
Only other Mag 7 member beating market, up 68% YTD. Reports Wednesday—expect beat & raise per usual. Waymo just raised $16B at $126B valuation (was anchor, now industry-leading self-driving).
Reporting stunning quarter with Gemini 3 platform at 750M monthly average users; spending 175-185B on capex to win AI race; worth every penny.

$175–185B capex (vs 116.5B expected) but beat earnings; winning AI on consumer (Google, Cloud, Gemini, YouTube, Waze); best at everything; down ~3% after outperformance—due for breather; buy on dips for Charitable Trust.
Rumors of Google weakness, Gemini struggling vs Anthropic/ChatGPT; Cramer bought for travel trust—get YouTube, Waymo, search, Chrome, Gemini; now +2%; ignore pessimism.
Eric's portfolio: crushing it; data center, YouTube, everywhere; don't mess with it; own it.
Dinged last year; has come all the way back; overblown concerns about eating Nvidia's lunch or Nvidia's market share loss never fully realized.
Owns viable quantum computing business; held in Charitable Trust; quantum remains viable only at scale-stage majors.
Gemini lagging in alternative data; Cramer uses it but it's in the scrum of AI tools; not as strong as Claude; recommended for long-term college portfolio alongside core dividend stocks.

Caller Bill finished position at $275, up $60 from entry. Cramer: HOLD and ride; sellers will win eventually but stock goes to $400. 'You win on both'—Alphabet gains plus Celtics (joke about Boston bias).
Mosaic unparalleled: Google Search, Gemini, YouTube, Wemo, Google Cloud. Outstanding numbers expected.
Reports Wednesday; critical hyperscaler capex signal. Will they continue spending or see returns?
Big Four earnings tomorrow (Wed); charitable trust owns; watch capex commitment vs. cash flow.
Crushed earnings in all lines: Google Cloud extraordinary, YouTube strong again, search incredibly strong and funding buildout. Own it, don't trade it.

22% revenue growth, 82% EPS growth, Google Cloud +63% to $20B, custom TPU demand strong, Gemini Enterprise paid users +40% QoQ, raising CapEx by $5B to $180–190B with more expected in 2027; coining money, clear line of sight to profitability.
Anthropic reported buying $200B in chips and cloud storage from Alphabet, sending stock soaring after bell; client validation of data center investment thesis.
Magnificent Seven member owned by Cramer's charitable trust. Original FANG stock (Google); proven secular growth leader with rate-proof fundamentals; dominates search and cloud computing.
Important AI capabilities; mentioned as context for tech dominance but not specifically recommended for action.
Owns chips but Cramer likes all three (Nvidia, Amazon, Alphabet) for opportunity scale; not Jensen's preference but still major AI customer; deep pockets to invest.
Successfully raised $45B of planned $85B secondary offering despite market volatility; Berkshire Hathaway's $10B participation validates strong execution by Goldman Sachs.
